title: Bash Script to Go CLI Migration
description: Replace 6 bash scripts with 4 Go CLI commands for cross-platform support, eliminating jq/bash/sed/grep dependencies. This is Stream 2 of the 3-stream SDD alignment effort.

title: Setup Phase
description: Initialize project structure and dependencies for bash CLI migration
design: Create pkg/cli/spec/ and pkg/cli/context/ package directories. Ensure Cobra and go-git/v5 dependencies available. Verify template files exist in pkg/embedded/templates/.

title: Foundational: Core Packages
description: Create foundational packages (pkg/cli/spec/, pkg/cli/context/) that all user stories depend on. Must complete before any user story implementation.
design: Files: pkg/cli/spec/detector.go (FeatureContext, DetectFeatureContext), pkg/cli/spec/paths.go (path resolution), pkg/cli/spec/branch.go (branch name generation). Dependencies: Reuses pkg/cli/git/git.go functions. Based on common.sh analysis from research.md.

title: US1: sl spec info Command
description: As an AI agent, I need sl spec info to get feature paths and prerequisite validation so I can verify feature state before proceeding. Replaces check-prerequisites.sh.
design: Files: pkg/cli/commands/spec.go (parent command), pkg/cli/commands/spec_info.go (implementation). Flags: --json, --require-plan, --require-tasks, --include-tasks, --paths-only. Outputs: FEATURE_DIR, BRANCH, FEATURE_SPEC, AVAILABLE_DOCS. Reuses pkg/cli/spec/detector.go.
acceptance_criteria: sl spec info --json outputs valid JSON with FEATURE_DIR. --require-plan flag errors when plan.md missing. --require-tasks flag errors when tasks.md missing. --include-tasks adds tasks.md to AVAILABLE_DOCS. --paths-only outputs minimal paths JSON.

title: US2: sl spec create Command
description: As a developer, I need sl spec create to generate a feature branch and spec directory so I can start a new feature without bash scripts. Replaces create-new-feature.sh.
design: Files: pkg/cli/commands/spec_create.go (implementation), pkg/cli/spec/branch.go (GenerateBranchName with stop-words). Logic: Stop-word filtering (the, a, an, etc.), acronym preservation (OAuth2, API), 244-byte truncation (GitHub limit). Outputs: BRANCH_NAME, FEATURE_DIR, SPEC_FILE, FEATURE_NUM. Uses go-git/v5 for branch operations.
acceptance_criteria: Branch NNN-short-name created from number and name. Stop-words filtered from descriptions. Acronyms preserved. Branch name truncated to 244 bytes. JSON output contains all required fields. Collision detection with existing features.

title: US3: sl spec setup-plan Command
description: As a developer, I need sl spec setup-plan to copy plan templates so I can begin planning without bash scripts. Replaces setup-plan.sh.
design: Files: pkg/cli/commands/spec_setup_plan.go (implementation). Logic: Copy template from pkg/embedded/templates/.specledger/templates/plan-template.md to feature directory. Error if plan.md already exists. Output PLAN_FILE path in JSON mode.
acceptance_criteria: plan.md created from template in feature directory. Error with message when plan.md already exists. JSON output contains PLAN_FILE path.

title: US4: sl context update Command
description: As a developer, I need sl context update to parse plan.md and update agent files so AI assistants have current feature context. Replaces update-agent-context.sh.
design: Files: pkg/cli/commands/context.go (parent), pkg/cli/commands/context_update.go (implementation), pkg/cli/context/parser.go (ParseTechnicalContext), pkg/cli/context/updater.go (marker preservation, deduplication). Logic: Parse Technical Context from plan.md, update agent file (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, etc.), preserve content between MANUAL ADDITIONS markers, deduplicate entries. Support 17+ agent types.
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE.md Active Technologies updated from plan.md. Manual additions preserved between markers. Duplicate entries deduplicated. Multiple agent types supported via --agent flag. JSON output contains updated file paths.

title: US5: Cross-Platform Support
description: As a Windows developer, I need all sl commands to work without bash so I can use SpecLedger on any platform.
design: Files: All pkg/cli/commands/*.go files. Requirements: Use filepath.Join() for all paths, no shell invocation, use go-git/v5 for git ops, ensure identical JSON output across platforms. Test on macOS, Linux, Windows.
acceptance_criteria: All 4 commands work on Windows without bash/jq/sed/grep errors. Path separators correct for each platform. JSON output identical across platforms. No shell invocation in code paths.

title: Polish & Cross-Cutting Concerns
description: Final polish, documentation updates, and integration testing after all user stories complete.
design: Tasks: Update README with new commands, add usage examples, verify all commands work together, test AI command integration (deferred to 599), cleanup bash scripts (keep as fallback).
acceptance_criteria: Documentation updated with new CLI commands. All commands tested together. Integration tests pass. Bash scripts retained as fallback.

title: Verify pkg/cli/spec/ directory structure
description: WHY: Need directory structure in place before creating detector.go, paths.go, and branch.go files.
design: Create pkg/cli/spec/ directory if it does not exist. Ensure proper package structure with Go files.
acceptance_criteria: Directory pkg/cli/spec/ exists and is ready for Go source files.

title: Verify pkg/cli/context/ directory structure
description: WHY: Need directory structure in place before creating parser.go and updater.go files.
design: Create pkg/cli/context/ directory if it does not exist. Ensure proper package structure with Go files.
acceptance_criteria: Directory pkg/cli/context/ exists and is ready for Go source files.

title: Verify Cobra and go-git/v5 dependencies
description: WHY: These are core dependencies for CLI commands and git operations.
design: Check go.mod for Cobra and go-git/v5 dependencies. Verify they are properly installed. Add if missing.
acceptance_criteria: Cobra and go-git/v5 are available in go.mod and properly installed.

title: Verify template files exist
description: WHY: Commands need access to plan-template.md and spec-template.md for file generation.
design: Verify pkg/embedded/templates/.specledger/templates/plan-template.md exists. Verify spec-template.md exists. Check embedded file access patterns.
acceptance_criteria: Template files exist in expected location and are accessible via embedded FS.

title: Create pkg/cli/spec/detector.go
description: WHY: All commands need to detect feature context from current branch. Replaces get_repo_root(), get_current_branch(), get_feature_paths() from common.sh.
design: File: pkg/cli/spec/detector.go. Struct: FeatureContext{RepoRoot, Branch, FeatureDir, SpecFile, PlanFile, TasksFile, HasGit}. Function: DetectFeatureContext() (*FeatureContext, error). Logic: Use go-git/v5 to get repo root and current branch. Parse branch name to extract feature number. Generate feature paths. Handle detached HEAD error.
acceptance_criteria: DetectFeatureContext() returns FeatureContext with all fields populated. Works with git repositories. Returns error on detached HEAD. Reuses pkg/cli/git/git.go functions.

title: Create pkg/cli/spec/paths.go
description: WHY: All commands need to resolve feature directory paths in a cross-platform way.
design: File: pkg/cli/spec/paths.go. Functions: GetFeatureDir(repoRoot, branch string) string, GetSpecFile(featureDir string) string, GetPlanFile(featureDir string) string, GetTasksFile(featureDir string) string, DiscoverDocs(featureDir string) ([]string, error). Logic: Use filepath.Join() for cross-platform paths. Check file existence with os.Stat(). Return list of available docs.
acceptance_criteria: All path functions use filepath.Join(). Paths work on macOS, Linux, Windows. DiscoverDocs() returns list of .md files in feature directory.

title: Create pkg/cli/spec/branch.go
description: WHY: sl spec create needs branch name generation with stop-word filtering and 244-byte truncation.
design: File: pkg/cli/spec/branch.go. Constants: MaxBranchLength = 244. Variables: StopWords map[string]bool (full list from research.md). Functions: GenerateBranchName(description string, number int) string, FilterStopWords(words []string) []string, PreserveAcronyms(original, word string) string, TruncateToLimit(name string, maxBytes int) string. Logic: Split description into words, filter stop-words, preserve acronyms, take 3-4 meaningful words, truncate to 244 bytes preserving feature number prefix.
acceptance_criteria: Stop-words filtered correctly. Acronyms preserved (OAuth2, API, JWT). Branch name truncated to 244 bytes max. Feature number prefix preserved during truncation.

title: Create pkg/cli/commands/spec.go parent command
description: WHY: Need parent command for all sl spec subcommands (info, create, setup-plan).
design: File: pkg/cli/commands/spec.go. Function: NewSpecCmd() *cobra.Command. Logic: Create parent command with Use: spec, Short: Manage feature specifications, add subcommands in init() or main.
acceptance_criteria: Parent command spec created. Ready to accept subcommands (info, create, setup-plan).

title: Create pkg/cli/commands/spec_info.go implementation
description: WHY: Implements sl spec info command to replace check-prerequisites.sh with JSON output and validation flags.
design: File: pkg/cli/commands/spec_info.go. Struct: SpecInfoOutput{FeatureDir, Branch, FeatureSpec, AvailableDocs []string}. Function: NewSpecInfoCmd() *cobra.Command. Flags: --json, --require-plan, --require-tasks, --include-tasks, --paths-only. Logic: Detect feature context, check required files, discover available docs, output JSON or text. Use pkg/cli/spec/detector.go and pkg/cli/spec/paths.go.
acceptance_criteria: JSON output with FEATURE_DIR, BRANCH, FEATURE_SPEC. --require-plan errors when plan.md missing. --require-tasks errors when tasks.md missing. --include-tasks adds tasks.md to AVAILABLE_DOCS. --paths-only outputs minimal JSON.

title: Register spec commands in cmd/sl/main.go
description: WHY: Commands must be registered to be available in the sl CLI.
design: File: cmd/sl/main.go. Logic: Import pkg/cli/commands package. Add specCmd to root command in init() or main(). Ensure command hierarchy: sl -> spec -> info.
acceptance_criteria: sl spec info command available and functional. Command accessible from CLI.

title: Create pkg/cli/commands/spec_create.go implementation
description: WHY: Implements sl spec create command to replace create-new-feature.sh with branch creation and spec directory setup.
design: File: pkg/cli/commands/spec_create.go. Struct: SpecCreateOutput{BranchName, FeatureDir, SpecFile, FeatureNum}. Function: NewSpecCreateCmd() *cobra.Command. Flags: --json, --number, --short-name. Logic: Generate branch name with pkg/cli/spec/branch.go, create branch with go-git/v5, create spec directory, copy spec template, output JSON. Check for collision with existing features.
acceptance_criteria: Branch NNN-short-name created from number and name. Stop-words filtered. Acronyms preserved. Branch name truncated to 244 bytes. JSON output contains all required fields. Collision detection works.

title: Implement feature number collision detection
description: WHY: Prevent creating features with duplicate numbers which would cause confusion and git conflicts.
design: File: pkg/cli/spec/collision.go (new file) or add to detector.go. Function: CheckFeatureCollision(repoRoot, featureNum string) error. Logic: Check specledger/ directory for existing features with same number. Check local git branches. Check remote branches (if available). Return error with collision message if found.
acceptance_criteria: Detects collision with existing local features. Detects collision with existing branches. Returns clear error message.

title: Create pkg/cli/commands/spec_setup_plan.go implementation
description: WHY: Implements sl spec setup-plan command to replace setup-plan.sh with template copying functionality.
design: File: pkg/cli/commands/spec_setup_plan.go. Struct: SpecSetupPlanOutput{PlanFile}. Function: NewSpecSetupPlanCmd() *cobra.Command. Flags: --json. Logic: Detect feature context, check if plan.md exists (error if so), copy template from pkg/embedded/templates/.specledger/templates/plan-template.md to feature directory, output JSON with PLAN_FILE path.
acceptance_criteria: plan.md created from template in feature directory. Error with message when plan.md already exists. JSON output contains PLAN_FILE path.

title: Create pkg/cli/context/parser.go
description: WHY: Need to parse Technical Context section from plan.md to extract metadata for agent files.
design: File: pkg/cli/context/parser.go. Struct: TechnicalContext{Language, Framework, Storage, ProjectType}. Function: ParseTechnicalContext(planPath string) (*TechnicalContext, error). Logic: Read plan.md, find Technical Context section, parse key-value pairs (Language/Version, Primary Dependencies, Storage, Project Type). Use strings and regexp for parsing. Log warning on malformed fields, skip them.
acceptance_criteria: ParseTechnicalContext() extracts all fields from plan.md. Handles malformed metadata gracefully. Returns error on missing plan.md.

title: Create pkg/cli/context/updater.go
description: WHY: Need to update agent files with plan metadata while preserving manual additions and deduplicating entries.
design: File: pkg/cli/context/updater.go. Struct: AgentUpdater{AgentType, FilePath}. Functions: Update(ctx *TechnicalContext) error, PreserveManualAdditions(content string) string, DeduplicateEntries(entries []string) []string. Logic: Read agent file, extract content between MANUAL ADDITIONS markers, generate new Active Technologies section from TechnicalContext, deduplicate entries, reassemble file with preserved manual additions, write atomically.
acceptance_criteria: Agent files updated with plan metadata. Manual additions preserved between markers. Duplicate entries deduplicated. Marker format: <!-- MANUAL ADDITIONS START --> and <!-- MANUAL ADDITIONS END -->.

title: Create pkg/cli/commands/context.go parent command
description: WHY: Need parent command for all sl context subcommands (update).
design: File: pkg/cli/commands/context.go. Function: NewContextCmd() *cobra.Command. Logic: Create parent command with Use: context, Short: Manage agent context files, add subcommands in init() or main.
acceptance_criteria: Parent command context created. Ready to accept subcommands (update).

title: Create pkg/cli/commands/context_update.go implementation
description: WHY: Implements sl context update command to replace update-agent-context.sh with agent file updates.
design: File: pkg/cli/commands/context_update.go. Struct: ContextUpdateOutput{UpdatedFiles []string}. Function: NewContextUpdateCmd() *cobra.Command. Flags: --json, --agent (default: claude). Logic: Parse plan.md with pkg/cli/context/parser.go, determine agent file path from agent type (17+ mappings from research.md), update agent file with pkg/cli/context/updater.go, output JSON with updated files. Agent mappings: claude->CLAUDE.md, gemini->GEMINI.md, copilot->.github/agents/copilot-instructions.md, cursor->.cursor/rules/specify-rules.mdc, etc.
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE.md Active Technologies updated from plan.md. Manual additions preserved. Duplicates deduplicated. Multiple agent types supported via --agent flag. JSON output contains updated file paths.

title: Verify all path operations use filepath package
description: WHY: Ensure cross-platform compatibility by using filepath.Join() instead of string concatenation with / or \
design: Files: All pkg/cli/**/*.go files. Logic: Search for path string concatenation patterns (fmt.Sprintf with /, + with /). Replace with filepath.Join(). Ensure no hardcoded path separators.
acceptance_criteria: All path operations use filepath.Join(). No hardcoded path separators. Code review confirms cross-platform path handling.

title: Test commands on Windows platform
description: WHY: Verify commands work without bash/jq/sed/grep errors on Windows.

items.item: sl spec info works on Windows
sl spec create works on Windows
sl spec setup-plan works on Windows
sl context update works on Windows

notes: Windows testing guide created at: specledger/600-bash-cli-migration/windows-testing-guide.md. Requires manual testing on Windows platform. All code verified to be cross-platform compatible (no bash/jq/grep/sed dependencies, all paths use filepath.Join()).
design: Platform: Windows (CI or manual). Commands: sl spec info, sl spec create, sl spec setup-plan, sl context update. Logic: Run all 4 commands on Windows, verify no bash/jq/sed/grep errors, verify correct path separators, verify identical JSON output.
acceptance_criteria: All 4 commands work on Windows without errors. No bash/jq/sed/grep dependencies. Path separators correct. JSON output identical to macOS/Linux.

title: Verify JSON output identical across platforms
description: WHY: Ensure AI agents can parse JSON output consistently regardless of platform.

items.item: JSON output captured on all platforms

design: Platform: macOS, Linux, Windows. Commands: All 4 commands with --json flag. Logic: Run commands on all platforms, capture JSON output, compare for differences (should be identical except file paths). Verify JSON is valid and parseable.
acceptance_criteria: JSON output format identical across platforms. JSON is valid and parseable. Only file paths differ (which is expected).

title: Update documentation with new CLI commands
description: WHY: Users need to know about the new sl spec and sl context commands and how to use them.
design: Files: README.md, docs/CLI.md (if exists). Content: Add sl spec info, sl spec create, sl spec setup-plan, sl context update to documentation. Include command descriptions, flags, examples, JSON output format.
acceptance_criteria: README.md updated with new commands. Command descriptions clear. Flags documented. JSON output examples provided.

title: Add usage examples for each command
description: WHY: Provide concrete examples to help users understand command usage.
design: Files: README.md, docs/examples.md (if exists). Content: Add 2-3 examples per command showing common use cases. Examples: sl spec info --json, sl spec create --number 600 --short-name test-feature, sl context update claude.
acceptance_criteria: Each command has 2-3 usage examples. Examples cover common use cases. Examples are copy-pasteable.

title: Verify all commands work together
description: WHY: Ensure end-to-end workflow works: create spec -> setup plan -> update context.
design: Commands: sl spec create, sl spec info, sl spec setup-plan, sl context update. Logic: Run full workflow: create new feature, check info, setup plan, update context. Verify each step succeeds and outputs correct data.
acceptance_criteria: Full workflow completes successfully. Each command produces expected output. No errors in workflow.

title: Retain bash scripts as fallback
description: WHY: Keep bash scripts during transition period until AI commands updated in 599-alignment.
design: Files: .specledger/scripts/bash/*.sh. Logic: Add deprecation notice to bash scripts. Keep scripts functional. Document that Go CLI commands are preferred. Plan removal in 599-alignment.
acceptance_criteria: Bash scripts retained. Deprecation notices added. Scripts still functional. Documentation mentions Go CLI is preferred.
